blocks.content: On the fifth day since Shirone's death.
All the students had regained their memories.
The children were particularly confused.
But since they were also reincarnates with exceptional mental resilience compared to ordinary people, the aftereffects weren't as severe as expected.
But the real challenge was just beginning.
The incident where the school was occupied by monsters exposed the vulnerabilities in its security systems.
Especially for a school operating a dormitory system, safety was the top priority.
The biggest problem was that a student had died.
And not just any student—Arian Shirone, one of the school's most promising talents.
At Iruki and Neid's request, Shirone was placed in the infirmary instead of the morgue.
Common sense dictated that a funeral should be held, but the faculty were hesitant.
The reason the student council hadn't yet moved to assign blame was that Shirone's death hadn't been officially confirmed.
In essence, they were planning to avoid responsibility by turning Shirone into a ghost.
Of course, many teachers also felt pangs of conscience about this approach.
Currently, they were gathered in the faculty meeting room, emphasizing the urgency of the situation.

blocks.speaker: Headmaster Alpheas
blocks.content: We must hold Shirone's funeral immediately. We also need to inform the parents. If we don't handle this now, the school's policies will be criticized in the future.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: This isn't something to decide lightly. If word gets out that a student has died, the repercussions will be enormous. We could lose the reputation we've built over the years in an instant.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: So you're suggesting we lie and say the dead student is alive? It'll come out eventually! The school's stance now should be to make a conscientious declaration. The issue isn't reputation—the school might have to close its doors.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Who doesn't know that? We're just saying we need a little more time to think.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Why can't you see that the longer we delay, the worse it gets? Currently, even Arcane's disciples are missing. Letting criminals escape is also a critical issue. We need to act now.
The teachers delaying Shirone's funeral were divided into two factions.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Headmaster, please speak! I heard the student council is convening today. The faculty must also take action.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: I'm in favor of observing.
Most of the teachers stood up.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Headmaster!
Alpheas understood their feelings.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: The longer they delayed, the worse the school's position became.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: But for him, something was more important than the school's survival: determining Shirone's exact condition.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Was Shirone dead?
It was an absurd agenda to propose at a magic school, the symbol of intellect.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: That made him even more cautious.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: If the deceased hadn't been Shirone, would they have even entertained such doubts?

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: Let's not jump to conclusions about Shirone. We're currently lacking information about Unlockers.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Shirone entered the Immortal Function three months ago. And in this battle, he opened it to an extreme degree.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The monastic order recognizes the Immortal Function as a process of spiritual sublimation.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: It might be a matter separate from life.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: What nonsense is that? We're not here to listen to the monastic order's stance.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: We want to resolve this situation as quickly as possible.
Some teachers didn't understand Ethela's words.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Ethela had been rebuked by a teacher who spoke directly to Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Headmaster, change your mind.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Frankly, this all started because of your personal grudges.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: For us, nothing is more important than the school.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: The worst? What exactly do you think the worst is?

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The school might be ordered to close.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: No, the worst isn't that.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The worst for a school is when all its students and teachers die.
The meeting room fell silent.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: If it weren't for Shirone, no one would have survived.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: And that list of casualties would have included all of you.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Alpheas was disappointed by the teachers' tendency to exploit a student's death rather than honor it.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Isn't there even a sliver of possibility left?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Even if it's less than one percent, didn't that child give his life for us?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Then shouldn't we at least try until the very end?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Even if the school closes, isn't it our duty as teachers to do everything we can to bring Shirone back?

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: But Headmaster, what is the school to us?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I'll take full responsibility.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: So please, work for Shirone's sake.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Shouldn't we at least make this sacrifice for that child?
The teachers couldn't refute Alpheas's words.
Whatever the reason, the fact that they owed their lives to a student was undeniable.
With that, the meeting ended, and the teachers left the room with dark expressions.
The Magic School Student Council.
Representatives from each class gathered in the large meeting room.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: Greetings. I am Amira, the student council president. I've convened this meeting to discuss the current situation. Please share constructive opinions.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Seriel from Class Three. I'm aware of the serious criticism against Headmaster Alpheas within the school. But I believe we must be cautious.

blocks.speaker: Kas
blocks.content: Kas from Class Four. Seriel's opinion has merit, but this agenda concerns the faculty's morality.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Olivia from Class Six. The main issue now is whether Shirone is dead.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: We know Shirone is dead.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: But a minority of students and teachers are distorting that fact.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: Because of this, the school has let two suspects allied with Arcane escape, and the headmaster's indictment is being delayed.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Aren't you overinterpreting the faculty's intentions?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Let's not jump to conclusions about Shirone. We're currently lacking information about Unlockers.

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: Shirone entered the Immortal Function three months ago. And in this battle, he opened it to an extreme degree.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The monastic order recognizes the Immortal Function as a process of spiritual sublimation.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: It might be a matter separate from life.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What nonsense is that?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We're not here to listen to the monastic order's stance.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: We want to resolve this situation as quickly as possible.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Shirone's heart isn't beating. None of you here fail to miss Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Isn't that right?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Everyone misses Shirone and is grateful to him.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: So let me ask again. Does anyone here truly believe Shirone is alive?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Of course, no one does.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's not cruel—it's common sense. Yet the faculty still insist Shirone is alive. Like a cult.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: That's not true! Shirone's condition is clearly unusual……!

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: What exactly is unusual?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I've read the report on the Immortal Function. But the state of magic has nothing to do with biological death.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I've also received intelligence that Shirone's body has been embalmed.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: That, that's……!

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: Embalmed? Is Fermi's claim true?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: I invoke my right to remain silent.
Amira didn't delay.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: If the tide was turning, now was the best time to act.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: She called for a vote.
The result…… was as Seriel expected.
Alpheas must resign! Submit to the disciplinary committee's investigation!
Hold Shirone's funeral! Playing with the dead is no act for a teacher!
A hundred students, aligned with the student council's decision, held up picket signs and protested in front of the infirmary.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: The school must disclose the full details of this incident. Indicting Headmaster Alpheas is the only way to secure the magic school's future!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Right! Alpheas must resign! Hold Shirone's funeral!
Their shouts reached Amy and the others through the infirmary window.
Then the door burst open, and Seriel rushed in.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy, it's bad!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What's going on? Why are they suddenly like this?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: The graduating class is conspiring to indict the headmaster.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I thought we could at least protect Shirone, but Fermi stirred up chaos…….

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy could guess the situation without seeing it.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Fermi was more than capable of such mischief.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: The student council was moving to oust the headmaster.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But she couldn't blame them.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: If the school's leader had moral flaws, cleaning house was good for the future.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But this was too extreme.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's why Fermi's antics were annoying.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: While the faculty's weak response had ignited the student council's anger, Fermi had lit the fuse.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That jerk. He owes his life to Shirone too.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What do we do, Amy?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy bit her nails.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What can we do? We hold out.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Happy now? A mob of kids who think like you is here.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Want me to join them?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Shouldn't you be out there with them?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Tch! You think Iruki here will cooperate?

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Enough. Fighting among ourselves won't solve anything.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: We need to unite now.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Wasn't planning to leave anyway.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: The infirmary's atmosphere grew icy.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Everyone was on edge, worn down by the situation.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy stroked Shirone's forehead.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Common sense said he was dead.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But clinging to hope now—was that even normal?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Please, please come back, Shirone…….

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy couldn't be sure Shirone would return.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But with his heart stopped for five days, certainty was long past.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Now it was a matter of faith.
— Twelve years later —
